exercises.title: @TempDir lifecycle
exercises.scenario: @TempDir is auto-created and auto-deleted by JUnit.
exercises.expected_solution:
true
hello
File is deleted after test (no manual cleanup needed)

test_cases.expected:
true
hello
File is deleted after test (no manual cleanup needed)

hints: Files.writeString writes to the temp directory
tempDir and all its contents are auto-deleted after the test
Safe: no cleanup code needed, no leftover files

exercises.title: Write and assert a test file
exercises.scenario: Write CSV to @TempDir, read result, assert count.
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks to complete this file-based test.

blanks.hint: JUnit annotation for temporary directory injection
Creates a path inside the temp dir

hints: @TempDir Path tempDir — inject the temp dir
tempDir.resolve('filename') — path inside the dir
Files.writeString(path, content)

exercises.title: StringReader for BufferedReader-accepting methods
exercises.scenario: StringReader simulates a file without touching the filesystem.
exercises.instructions:
Write a test for `WordCounter.count(BufferedReader reader)` that counts non-blank lines. Use StringReader — no file needed.

Expected test input: 'hello\n\nworld\nfoo'
Expected: 3 non-blank lines
hints: new BufferedReader(new StringReader(text)) — no file needed
try-with-resources to properly close the reader

Input 'hello

world
foo' has 4 lines, 1 blank → 3 non-blank

exercises.title: Test file writer — assert output content
exercises.scenario: Write content via service, read back from @TempDir, assert.
exercises.instructions:
Test `LogWriter.writeLines(Path file, List<String> lines)` appends each line. Verify file has correct content.

Expected: written lines match read lines.
hints: Files.readAllLines(logFile) after writing
assert count then specific lines
assertTrue(Files.exists(logFile)) first if defensive

exercises.title: Mock HttpResponse and HttpClient
exercises.scenario: Stub HttpClient to return a mock response without a server.
exercises.instructions:
Write a test for `StockService.fetchPrice(String symbol)` which calls `httpClient.send()` and parses the JSON body. Mock both HttpClient and HttpResponse.

Expected: fetchPrice('TCS') returns 3890.
hints: when(httpResponse.body()).thenReturn(jsonString)
when(httpClient.send(any(), any())).thenReturn(httpResponse)
No statusCode stub needed if service doesn't check it

exercises.title: Test all HTTP status codes
exercises.scenario: Test 200, 404, and 500 responses with different mock stubs.
exercises.instructions:
Write 3 tests for `UserApiClient.getUser(String id)` that returns Optional<String> (name) or Optional.empty() on 404, and throws RuntimeException on 500.

Mock all three scenarios.
hints: Stub statusCode first, body for 200 only
Each test creates a fresh UserApiClient
404 doesn't need body stub — switch returns empty before reading body

exercises.title: Test CSV parser with real file in @TempDir
exercises.scenario: Full CSV parsing test: write file, parse, assert result.
exercises.instructions:
Write 4 tests for `CSVImporter.importProducts(Path)` using @TempDir:
1. Valid CSV → correct products
2. Header-only → empty list
3. Non-existent file → IOException
4. Quoted field with comma → parsed correctly
hints: One @TempDir per test method — each gets its own clean dir
Files.writeString(f, csvContent) to set up test data
assertThrows for non-existent file (IOException or NoSuchFileException)

exercises.title: StringWriter for report generator test
exercises.scenario: Test formatted output without creating files.
exercises.instructions:
Test `InvoiceGenerator.generate(PrintWriter writer, List<Order> orders)` using StringWriter. Assert the output contains header, order lines, and total.

Expected output contains:
=== Invoice ===
Total: ₹[sum]
[order lines]
hints: StringWriter sw = new StringWriter(); PrintWriter pw = new PrintWriter(sw)
After gen.generate(pw, orders): sw.toString() has full output

exercises.title: Test HTTP network error path
exercises.scenario: Mock HttpClient to throw IOException for network failures.
exercises.instructions:
Write a test for `WeatherClient.getTemperature(String city)` that returns -1 on IOException (network failure). Mock the client to throw.

Expected: IOException → returns -1
hints: when(client.send(any(), any())).thenThrow(new IOException())
Service catches IOException and returns -1
Test verifies graceful degradation

exercises.title: @TempDir scope — per-test vs shared
exercises.scenario: Per-method @TempDir is isolated; shared (static) @TempDir persists across tests.
hints: static @TempDir: one directory for the whole class
method @TempDir: unique directory per test method
The two methodDirs are also different from each other

exercises.title: Test a full log analyser — read file, write report
exercises.scenario: Full read → process → write pipeline test.
exercises.instructions:
Write 3 tests for `LogAnalyser.analyse(Path logFile, Path reportFile)`:
1. 3 ERROR lines → report contains 'ERROR count: 3'
2. No errors → report contains 'ERROR count: 0'
3. Empty log → report still created with header
hints: Both input and output files in same @TempDir
Files.createFile(path) creates an empty file
Files.readString(report) for full content assertion

exercises.title: Test API client with ArgumentCaptor — verify URL
exercises.scenario: Capture the HttpRequest to verify the correct URL was used.
exercises.instructions:
Test that `ProductApiClient.searchProducts(String query, int limit)` builds the correct URL: `/api/products?q={query}&limit={limit}`.

Use ArgumentCaptor to capture the HttpRequest and assert its URI.
hints: @Captor ArgumentCaptor<HttpRequest> — capture the request
verify(client).send(captor.capture(), any())
captor.getValue().uri().toString() for URL assertion

exercises.title: Complete CSV processor test suite
exercises.scenario: Full test coverage for a CSV-to-JSON transformer.
exercises.instructions:
Write 5 tests for `CSVToJSONConverter.convert(Path csvPath, Path jsonPath)` using @TempDir:
1. Valid 3-row CSV → JSON array with 3 objects
2. Header-only → empty JSON array
3. Missing input file → IOException
4. Quoted fields with commas → correctly in JSON
5. Round-trip: convert CSV → read JSON back → assert data matches
hints: mapper.readTree(Files.readString(jsonPath)) to parse output
arr.size() for count, arr.get(0).path('name').asText() for field
Empty CSV produces '[]' — size() = 0

exercises.title: Test stock API client — full mock suite
exercises.scenario: Complete HTTP client test coverage with 5 scenarios.
exercises.instructions:
Write 5 tests for `ZerodhaStockClient.getQuote(String symbol)` returning `Optional<StockQuote>`:
1. HTTP 200 → parse JSON → return quote
2. HTTP 404 → empty Optional
3. HTTP 429 → throw RateLimitException
4. IOException → empty Optional (graceful)
5. Verify correct URL contains the symbol
hints: each test creates new ZerodhaStockClient(client) to avoid state
429 bypasses try-catch so the exception propagates
IOException caught inside getQuote() → returns empty

exercises.title: Module 12.4 synthesis — inventory sync system
exercises.scenario: File I/O + HTTP client in one test suite.
exercises.instructions:
Build and test `InventorySyncService.sync(Path csvPath)` which:
1. Reads product CSV from csvPath
2. For each product, POSTs to an API to update inventory
3. Writes a sync report to a 'sync-report.txt' file next to the CSV
4. Returns count of successfully synced products

Write 4 tests using @TempDir + mocked HttpClient:
- Valid CSV → correct count, report written, API called per product
- Empty CSV → 0 synced, report with 0 count
- API returns 500 → product counted as failed, continues others
- Verify correct API endpoint URL contains product SKU
hints: when(response.statusCode()).thenReturn(200, 500) — first call 200, second 500
csvPath.getParent().resolve('sync-report.txt') — report next to CSV
Both @TempDir and @Mock in the same test — they compose naturally
